---
title: "#30DayChartChallenge"
format: html
echo: false
warning: false
message: false
output:
    html_document:
        fig_retina: 1
---

# 30 Day Chart Challenge 2026
## Day 17 - Relationship: Remake

### Load libraries & Set Themes
```{r}
#| label: Load libraries & Set Themes
pacman::p_load(
  tidyverse,
  janitor,
  ggrepel,
  ggtext,
  stringr,
  scales,
  MetBrewer,
  lubridate,
  ggh4x
  # showtext
)

# showtext and ggtext::element_markdown 
# are not behaving properly together
#
# font_add_google(
#   "Hanken Grotesk",
#   "Hanken Grotesk",
#   regular.wt = 400,
#   bold.wt = 800
# )
# showtext_auto()
# showtext_opts(dpi = 300)

warm_white <- "#f0ebe3ff"
warm_black <- "#38352fff"

# MetBrewer::MetPalettes$Nizami
colors_nizami <- c(
  orange_light = "#dd7867",
  red          = "#b83326", 
  orange_dark  = "#c8570d",
  yellow       = "#edb144",
  teal         = "#8cc8bc",
  teal_dark    = "#67a397",
  blue_light   = "#7da7ea",
  blue         = "#5773c0", 
  blue_dark    = "#1d4497"
)

theme_set(
  theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      # axis.line.x.bottom = element_line(color = '#474747', linewidth = .3),
      # axis.ticks.x= element_line(color = '#474747', linewidth = .3),
      # axis.line.y.left = element_line(color = '#474747', linewidth = .3),
      # axis.ticks.y= element_line(color = '#474747', linewidth = .3),
      # # panel.grid = element_line(linewidth = .3, color = 'grey90'),
      panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.ticks.length = unit(-0.15, "cm"),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill = warm_white),
      plot.title.position = "plot",
      plot.caption.position = "plot",
      plot.title = element_markdown(
        family = "Hanken Grotesk",
        size = 12,
        hjust = 0,
        lineheight = 1.2,
        margin = margin(0, 0, 15, 0),
      ),
      plot.caption = element_markdown(
        family = "Hanken Grotesk",
        size = 8,
        color = 'grey60',
        margin = margin(15, 0, 0, 0),
        hjust = 0,
        lineheight = 1.2
      ),
      plot.subtitle = element_markdown(
        size = 9,
        lineheight = 1.2,
        margin = margin(5, 0, 0, 0),
        hjust = 0.5
      ),
      axis.title.x = element_markdown(
        family = "Hanken Grotesk",
        hjust = 0.5,
        size = 8,
        color = "grey40",
        margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0)
      ),
      axis.title.y = element_markdown(
        family = "Hanken Grotesk",
        hjust = 0.5,
        size = 8,
        color = "grey40",
        margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0)
      ),
      axis.text = element_markdown(
        family = "Hanken Grotesk",
        hjust = 0.5,
        size = 8,
        color = "grey40"
      ),
      legend.position = "top",
      text = element_text(family = "Hanken Grotesk", color = warm_black),
      plot.margin = margin(25, 25, 25, 25)
    )
)
```


### Read raw data

Original: https://github.com/barreiro-r/tidytuesday/tree/main/legacy/2021-08-10_bea
```{r}
# Loading data
tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load('2021-08-10')
```


### Preparing data for plot
```{r}
data2plot <-
  tuesdata$investment %>%
  filter(year > 2000, year <= 2020) %>%
  filter(
    category %in% c('Federal', 'Private') & !str_detect(meta_cat, 'Total')
  ) |>
  group_by(category, group_num, meta_cat) |>
  summarise(gross_inv = sum(gross_inv)) |>
  group_by(meta_cat) |>
  mutate(per = gross_inv / sum(gross_inv)) |>
  ungroup()
```


### Time for plotting
```{r fig.showtext=TRUE}
pacman::p_load(patchwork)

order <-
  data2plot |>
  select(category, meta_cat, per) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = category, values_from = per) |>
  mutate(Private = if_else(is.na(Private), 0, Private)) |>
  arrange(desc(Private)) |>
  pull(meta_cat)

my_plot <-
  data2plot |>
  mutate(meta_cat = factor(meta_cat, levels = order)) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = per, y = meta_cat)) +
  geom_col(
    aes(fill = category),
    position = 'stack',
    show.legend = FALSE,
    width = 1
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~meta_cat, ncol = 1, scales = 'free_y') +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), labels = scales::percent) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_blank(),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0, margin = margin(0, 0, 5, 0)),
    axis.title.y = element_blank()
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      'Private' = colors_nizami[["blue"]],
      'Federal' = colors_nizami[["yellow"]]
    ),
    labels = c('Private', 'Federal')
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL,
    fill = NULL,
    title = str_wrap(
      str_c(
        "**Funding the Foundation** The split between Private and Federal infrastructure spending from 2000 to 2020"
      ),
      width = 45,
      whitespace_only = FALSE
    ) |>
      glue::glue(
        "<br><br><span style='font-size:12px'>% of investiments (<span style='color:{colors_nizami[['blue']]}'>Private</span> / <span style='color:{colors_nizami[['yellow']]}'>Federal</span>)</span>"
      ) |>
      str_replace_all("\n", "<br>"),
    caption = str_wrap(
      width = 65,
      'SOURCE Bureau of Economic Analysis / TidyTuesday NOTE This visualization is a conceptual exercise focused on design and layout; it is intended as a stylistic study rather than a comprehensive or final analysis. GITHUB barreiro-r'
    ) |>
      str_replace_all("\n", "<br>"),
  )

ggsave(
  my_plot,
  width = 4,
  height = 5.5,
  filename = "2026/images/day17.png"
)
```
